terminologyProfile.descriptionRule: Use measurable 3D graphics terms. Avoid vague words unless they are paired with concrete geometry/material/shader parameters.

objectClass.notes: Showcase assessment authored from the user-provided reference.

reasoning: The vehicle is isolated and its raised four-wheel chassis, cabin, rear body, and wheel spacing are readable.
Brick studs, tire treads, and roof lamps are deterministic repetition systems.
The photographed near-side front wheel, rear wheel, hood, cabin, and roof cargo provide enough scale and overlap cues to infer the hidden pair and underbody.
The production hierarchy needs wheel and steering pivots, suspension anchors, hinged panels, roof sockets, collider proxies, and semantic destruction groups.

specDepthDecision.rationale: The README showcase must expose recognizable structure and controlled variation.

definitionOfDone: The reference-matched three-quarter render reads as the same olive brick-built off-road 4x4: long faceted hood, upright glazed cabin, light roof, rear utility body, raised arches, and exactly four wheels.
The full pass shows brick/stud language, tread blocks, hubs, suspension, panel seams, hinges, fasteners, grille, lamps, bumpers, roof rack, cargo, and warm recovery accents without imported meshes.
Generated olive paint, rubber, glazing, dark trim, metal, lamps, and dirt/wear use independent PBR channels and remain readable under neutral studio lighting.
Three deterministic configurations preserve component IDs, four-wheel topology, action-ready pivots, sockets, colliders, and destruction groups while varying bounded palette and surface-age controls.

featureGroups.name: Raised four-wheel off-road silhouette
qualityCriteria: Wheelbase, track, tire diameter, hood length, cabin height, roof overhang, and rear utility-body mass match the photographed proportions.
Exactly four wheels remain visible or inferable at the expected axle and track positions in every configuration.
failureModes: Short generic SUV proportions, five or more wheels, narrow track, centered cabin, or wheels floating outside the arches.

featureGroups.name: Four large tire and hub system
qualityCriteria: All four tires share axle orientation, tread cadence, black multi-spoke hubs, and suspension contact with the chassis.
Front steering pivots remain distinct from rear wheel pivots.
failureModes: Torus wheels face the wrong axis, duplicate wheels appear, tread blocks float, or hubs lack mechanical depth.

featureGroups.name: Cabin, hood, and rear utility-bed hierarchy
qualityCriteria: Faceted olive hood, black A-pillars, broad windshield, side windows, two doors, and rear utility body reproduce the photographed massing.
Light roof panels and black structural rails frame the glazed cabin.
failureModes: Monolithic body shell, missing windshield rake, missing doors, or rear body that reads as an open pickup bed.

featureGroups.name: Roof rack and lamp bar
qualityCriteria: Black roof rails support a dark cargo case, light recovery boards, fasteners, and warm auxiliary lamps at the photographed roof height.
failureModes: Floating roof cargo, oversized lamps, missing support rails, or cargo that obscures the light roof.

antiShallowSpecRules: Do not generate the showcase from a single undifferentiated mesh.
Do not vary protected component IDs, parent links, or review targets.
Do not accept variants without fresh visual evidence.
Reject any runtime that stores live Three.js references in serializable userData.
Reject a full pass that omits the photographed hood/cabin/window/door/fender/rear-body relationships.

mustMatch: Long faceted hood, upright glazed cabin, light roof, and rear utility-body proportions
Olive panels separated from black structure, rubber, glass, metal, and warm recovery accents
Brick studs, panel seams, tire tread, hubs, suspension, rack cargo, grille, and lamps
niceToHave: subtle edge wear and seam dirt
rear-most hidden-side details inferred from bilateral construction

visualAcceptance.scoringRule: AI vision must inspect a side-by-side reference/render sheet and score the current pass from 0 to 1. Pixel-diff code may assist diagnostics but cannot approve a pass.

featureReviewPolicy.selectionRule: Choose only the most visually salient, identity-defining, user-prioritized, or high-risk semantic systems. Group repeated parts instead of reviewing every mesh. AI vision scores every selected feature from the same full reference/render pair.

specRefineTriggers: local feature not traceable to viewEvidence
codeRefineTriggers: spec is adequate but generated geometry/material does not match
lighting hides geometry or material response
stopCriteria: target fidelity reached or user accepts current approximation
remaining gaps require new reference images or manual art

screenshotPolicy.minimumEvidence: Each visual pass needs a reference image, rendered screenshot, side-by-side comparison sheet, AI vision score, layer scores, and critique before choosing continue.
screenshotPolicy.reviewPairRule: Compare the same camera/viewpoint whenever possible; do not judge a front reference against a random render angle.
screenshotPolicy.acceptanceAuthority: AI vision review of the comparison sheet. Code-generated pixel similarity is not sufficient evidence.

featureReviewTargets.name: Reference wheelbase, track, ride height, and exactly four correctly oriented wheels
Faceted hood, upright cabin, windshield, side windows, doors, and light roof
Wheel arches, doors, rocker structure, rear utility body, bumpers, and suspension
Brick studs, tire tread blocks, hubs, seams, hinges, and visible fasteners
Olive paint, light roof, black trim, rubber, glass, metal, lamps, and dirt/wear remain physically distinct
Black roof rack, cargo case, recovery boards, auxiliary lamps, and warm accents

referencePbrExtraction.acceptedLimitation: single-image extraction is reference-derived inference, not exact photogrammetry

mustAvoid: plastic-looking smooth bark, stone, cloth, foliage, or aged material
local color/detail described only in prose without material masks
claiming exact PBR recovery when confidence is below the target threshold
reference lighting copied without separating material readability

screenshotReview: Compare albedo palette and local color zones.
Compare cavity dirt, edge wear, stains, moss, scratches, or other local masks.
Compare key/fill/rim structure, exposure, tone mapping, background, and contact shadows.
Capture a neutral-light render to verify material readability without reference lighting.
Capture a grazing-light close-up to expose flat normals, uniform roughness, tiling, and plastic highlights.
Capture a reference-matched render from the same camera framing as the source.

actionReadiness.contract: Every macro/meso component should be generated as a stable named Object3D pivot node with a mesh child, action metadata, optional sockets, collider proxy, and destruction metadata.
requiredComponentFields: attachment for child appendages, connectors, limbs, tubes, handles, legs, horns, wings, branches, or cables
authoringRules: Do not collapse independently movable parts into one mesh.
Put transforms on component pivot groups, not only on raw meshes.
For attached child parts, put the pivot at the semantic root/socket and build visible geometry from localStart to localEnd.
Represent hinge, socket, detachable, and breakable intent even when no animation is implemented yet.
Use simplified collider proxies for runtime physics instead of visual mesh colliders by default.

destructionPolicy.fractureGroupNaming: Use stable semantic names such as body-shell, left-hinge, glass-panel, branch-segment.
destructionPolicy.debrisStrategy: Prefer detachable component groups and a small number of procedural fragments over random mesh explosion.

assumptions: The hidden left-side wheel, suspension, door, and utility-body details mirror the visible near side while preserving four-wheel topology.
Fine branded text and exact commercial brick part topology are represented as procedural panel and fastener language rather than copied logos.
The single reference supports an approximate action-ready reconstruction, not an exact reverse-engineered production mesh.

coordinateFrame.front: negative X points toward the grille and long hood
coordinateFrame.scaleReference: one scene unit is approximately one stud module; wheel diameter is approximately 1.75 units

silhouette.boundingShape: long-wheelbase rectangular 4x4 with a low faceted hood, upright cabin, raised arches, and roof cargo
aspectRatios: overall length:width:height approximately 6.6:3.3:3.4 including tires and rack
silhouette.symmetry: bilateral across the longitudinal X/Y center plane with asymmetric roof cargo and visible-side wear
dominantCurves: arched olive fenders over each axle
rounded hood corners and light roof overhang
negativeSpaces: open wheel wells between tire and fender
dark window apertures bounded by black pillars
clearance below rocker panels and chassis
landmarks: long olive hood with raised center panel
wide black-framed windshield under a light roof
near-side front and rear doors with visible hinges and fasteners
black roof rack carrying dark case and light recovery boards

observations: The vehicle is isolated and its raised four-wheel chassis, cabin, rear body, and wheel spacing are readable.
Brick studs, tire treads, and roof lamps are deterministic repetition systems.
Hidden underbody details are simplified for a browser-real-time showcase.
The render reads as the same olive raised 4x4 expedition vehicle.
Three variants preserve four-wheel topology while changing panel palette and repeated-detail density.

runtimeConfiguration.evidencePolicy: All base blockout through final evidence uses the exact base configuration. Curated v001-v003 configurations are reserved for per-variant evidence.
runtimeConfiguration.surfaceAgeDriver: body-shell wear.edgeWear and dirt.amount are the shared runtime drivers for generated scratch, dirt, and roughness masks across all material families.

sculptDNA.authoringRule: These controls are conservative starters. Rename them in object language, add coupling groups for dependent proportions, and add ratio/range constraints before generating a production family.

repetitionSystems.name: Wheel tread blocks
constraints: equal front and rear wheel spacing

lodPlan.strategy: full component tree and material layers
merge static components and reduce local feature geometry

performanceBudget.optimizationPolicy: Preserve action-ready boundaries and the reference silhouette, instance dense repetition systems, and enforce renderer.info full-frame WebGL calls at or below 400.

lightingFromPhoto: Key light: soft sun or warm studio key with readable shadows.
Fill light: cool sky fill preserving material midtones.
Rim/environment light: subtle separation from the background.
Exposure and tone mapping: ACES Filmic with controlled highlights.
Contact shadow: ground and component-contact anchoring.
proceduralStrategy: Build macro silhouette before repeated details.
Use deterministic seeds for repeated systems.
Keep variant controls semantic and bounded.
Render three promoted variants from one camera for README comparison.

visualAcceptance.notes: The inspected v001 pair retains the olive/light-roof identity and strongest worn cavity response. The studio render is darker and less connector-dense than the source.
